title: initProject auto-copies defaults/schemas + defaults/blocks at init — violates DEC-0011 (ship no defaults) and is in tension with DEC-0025 (vocabulary-neutral).
description: initProject (index.ts:255-295) unconditionally copies defaults/schemas/* (14 schemas) + defaults/blocks/* (9 blocks) into a fresh substrate — imposing a conception at init. DEC-0011 (enacted) states pi-context ships NO default schemas or block files and users install desired schemas; this is a direct contradiction. There are THREE package schema channels — schemas/ (framework-contract, bundled, read by loadConfig), defaults/ (init-seed), registry/ (install catalog) — with defaults/ a subset of registry/ and divergence between them, a standing maintenance hazard. Both defaults/ and registry/ currently carry inline-FK schemas, so a fresh adopter’s substrate is born DEC-0013-violating the moment they init/install (this is the unit-2.2 concern). Intended outcome: init imposes nothing; a fresh substrate carries only what the user declares or accepts from the suggestion catalog — i.e. the defaults/ auto-seed is removed and superseded by the onboarding-elicitation ceremony (sibling FGAP).

evidence.reference: initProject resolves defaults/ dir then unconditionally copies defaults/schemas/* and defaults/blocks/* into the substrate — the conception-imposition at init
the two copy loops (schemas then blocks) that seed a fixed vocabulary regardless of user intent
DEC-0011 (enacted): pi-context does not ship default schemas or block files; users install desired schemas — directly contradicted. DEC-0025 vocabulary-neutral tension.

impact: Imposes a fixed vocabulary on every fresh project, contradicting enacted DEC-0011 and undercutting DEC-0025. Compounds with the three-channel divergence (defaults vs registry) as silent drift, and with inline-FK in shipped schemas (born-violating substrate). Must resolve in concert with the onboarding-elicitation FGAP (the elicitation is the replacement) and unit 2.2 (channel reconciliation + edge-based schemas).
proposed_resolution: Stop imposing a conception at init; the onboarding-elicitation ceremony (sibling FGAP) is the intended replacement, and registry/ becomes the suggestion catalog rather than an auto-default. Channel reconciliation (the defaults/ vs registry/ relationship) and exact removal mechanics are intentionally NOT prescribed here (outcome-only filing per user directive) — sequenced with the onboarding FGAP + unit 2.2 at the user’s direction.
